Items.Title: 快乐而富有成效的后台工程师 | Happy and Productive Engineers with Backstage
Items.Description:
蚂蚁（上）韦斯

发表于 6 月 28 日

内部交付门户 (IDP) 现在非常受欢迎。 这是可以理解的。 我们编写越来越多的软件，我们的系统变得更加模块化和复杂（有人说“微服务”吗？），我们的团队是自治和独立的。

但最终我们都需要共同努力，这就是故事的结局。 知识和交流同时无处不在，又无处不在——Jira 票据、Confluence 文章、CI/CD 系统、Git 存储库、Slack 通道、概念空间、Google 文档、Artifact 注册表，凡是你能想到的......

如果我们可以拥有一个网站/应用程序，我们可以随时找到所有相关信息和工具以高效工作，该怎么办？

我们长期以来一直有这个梦想，但现在越来越多的组织开始将其变为现实。

如果您想更好地了解这些门户的需求及其基本要求，请阅读此处。

有一个 OSS！


已经有许多供应商提供 IDP 解决方案，但我们知道我们的行业主要是由社区努力推动的。 这与 IDP 没有什么不同 - 有一个很棒的 OSS 项目，它已成为创建门户的事实上的行业标准！ 我说的是Backstage - 用于构建交付（开发人员）门户的框架。 它是在 Spotify 创建的，旨在改善工程部门内部的协作，然后开源 - 所以现在我们所有人都可以从中受益！

根据 Spotify 的说法，使用 Backstage 的效果是：

  • 工程师的入职时间减少了 55%。 入职时间是按照第 10 个拉取请求之前的时间来衡量的。

  • 所以是的 - 建立一个交付门户肯定会有回报。

    这里有越来越多的社区插件可用：

    野外后台 - 挑战

    去年，我们帮助许多客户实施了基于 Backstage 的 IDP。 入门绝对是具有挑战性的！
    如果您打算使用 Backstage 进行 IDP 工作，请注意以下一些陷阱：

    检查你的反应

    首先 - 整个应用程序是用 Typescript 编写的 Reactjs + Nodejs。 不完全是您选择的 DevOps 专业工具箱。 我们更加精通 Python、Go、Ruby 甚至 Java。
    事实上，语言本身并不是什么问题，但Reactjs是一个全新的世界。

    缺乏文档

    后台开发人员和社区在文档上投入了大量资金。 它们写得很漂亮。 但尽管如此——它们大多涵盖了基本的快乐之路。 一旦你追求更具冒险精神的东西 - 就很难理解什么是行不通的。 有几次，我们发现自己在 Discord 上联系后台开发人员，以找到给我们带来麻烦的隐藏配置字段。

    插件并不是真正的即插即用

    这太棒了！ 问题是 - 这些不是我们习惯的来自 Jenkins 或 Wordpress 等系统的插件。 为了集成插件，需要：

    • 修改后台核心代码
    • 重建整个代码库并
    • 重新部署他们的 IDP

    对于每月可能会更改多次的实时系统来说，这并不是完美的操作流程。 现在特别奇怪的是——我们已经了解了整体部署的痛苦以及如何通过模块化来减轻它们。

    升级很可怕

    因此，正如我们所说，配置插件和向 Backstage 添加任何其他更改基本上都涉及代码修改。 这有效地将您的 Backstage 安装变成了原始代码库的分支。 这使得每次升级都是由您执行的代码合并。 代码合并是......可怕的。
    后台开发人员甚至创建了一种工具来生成代码差异，需要手动将其集成到安装中才能升级。 我们对此非常感谢。 让事情变得更容易。 但还是很吓人。

    让每个人都登上舞台

    在克服了所有技术挑战之后，您就遇到了最困难的部分 - 组织采用。 每个 IDP 的核心都是软件目录 - 您的组织开发和使用的所有组件的注册表。 因此，为了有效采用，您需要：

    1. 将所有数据收集到 Backstage（将其转换为 Backstage 兼容格式）
    2. 说服所有工程师使用来自 IDP 的数据，而不是来自他们之前找到数据的任何地方。 不断更新数据——使其保持相关性和有用性。

    对于拥有数百名开发人员和数十个软件组件的大型组织来说，这并不是一件容易的事。 通常情况下，采用会失败或遇到挫折。 主要是因为组织中没有真正的 DX（开发人员经验）专业知识。 这需要非常具体的技能，而大多数地方都缺乏这种技能。

    因此，如果您一直在考虑在您的组织中推出 Backstage，请再考虑一下...或者 -

    使用后台发行版

    为了使 Backstage 的采用和日常操作对我们的客户来说更容易、更有价值，我们一直致力于开发自己的 Backstage 发行版，在 Kubernetes 上运行。

    目标是提供企业级产品：

    • 无缝部署体验
    • 自动目录发现
    • 无需重建的插件管理（针对主要插件）
    • 开箱即用的可管理 RBAC 和企业级审计
    • 具有可定制指标的集成可观察性。
    • 可选的报告和 Finops 扩展（一旦您将组织中的每个人都集中在一个地方，进行编目和记录 - 生成报告并相应地管理成本非常有意义）

    听起来不错？ 请随时在我们的网站上注册，以便在我们发布公共测试版时收到通知。

    如果国内流离失所者和后台的主题让您兴奋 - 订阅我的帖子。 以下是我计划讨论的一些主题：

    • 专有 IDP 产品（端口、Cortex、OpsLevel 等）的审查
    • 如何加入 Backstage OSS 社区并为 Backstage OSS 做出贡献
    • 有关如何在后台执行操作的技术帖子
    • 平台设计原理简介

    我还在 9 月份的瑞士云原生日举办后台研讨会，所以如果您在场，请报名参加！

    一旦被暂停，antweiss 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

    一旦解封，antweiss 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，antweiss 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

    如果 antweiss 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 antweiss：

    使 antweiss 的所有帖子不那么明显

    举报其他不当行为

    不标记 antweiss 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ant(on) Weiss

Posted on Jun 28

 

Internal Delivery Portals (IDPs) are all the rave now. (other folks call them Internal Developer Portals but I protest - they aren't only about developers!)

And it's understandable. We write more and more software, our systems become more modular and complex (did somebody say "microservices"?), our teams are autonomous and self-contained.

But at the end of the day we all need to join our efforts and that's where the story breaks up. The knowledge and communication is simultaneously everywhere and nowhere - Jira tickets, Confluence articles, CI/CD systems, Git repos, Slack channels, Notion spaces, Google docs, Artifact registries, you name it...

What if instead we could have a single website/app where we could always find all the relevant information and tools in order to work efficiently?

We've had this dream for a long time, but now more and more orgs are starting to make it a reality.

If you want a better understanding of the need for these portals and their basic requirements - read here.

There's an OSS for it!


There are already a number of vendors providing IDP solutions, but we know that our industry is mainly fueled by community efforts. It's no different with IDPs - there's a great OSS project that has become the de-facto industry standard for creating a portal! I'm talking about Backstage - the framework for building delivery (developer) portals. It was created at Spotify to improve the collaboration inside the engineering department and then open-sourced - so now we all can benefit from it!

According to Spotify the effects of using Backstage were:

  • A 55% decrease in onboarding time for their engineers. Onboarding time was measured by time until the 10th pull request.

  • 280 engineering teams inside Spotify used Backstage.

  • 2,000+ backend services, 300+ websites, 4,000+ data pipelines, and 200+ mobile features onboarded to Backstage.

(Taken from this paywalled post in Pragmatic Engineer newsletter)

So yes - building a delivery portal definitely pays off.

The added benefit of Backstage is that it's a flexible, extensible system - one can pretty easily develop custom integrations on top of it.

There's a growing number of community plugins available here:

backstage.io/plugins/

Backstage in the Wild - the Challenges

In the last year we've helped a number of our customers to implement an IDP based on Backstage. Getting started was definitely challenging!
Here are some of the pitfalls you should be aware of if you are looking at using Backstage for your IDP effort:

Check your Reaction

First of all - the whole app is Reactjs + Nodejs written in Typescript. Not exactly your DevOps professional toolbox of choice. We're much more proficient with Python, Go, Ruby or even Java.
In fact the language itself is less of a problem, but Reactjs is a whole new world to get into.

Documentation is Lacking

Backstage devs and community invested a lot in the docs. They are beautifully written. But still - they mostly cover the basic happy path. Once you go for something more adventurous - it's very hard to understand what's not working. A few times we found ourselves reaching out to Backstage devs on Discord in order to find that hidden config field that was giving us trouble.

Plugins aren't really Plug-n-Play

Backstage is based on the concept of plugins. Which is excellent! Trouble is - these aren't the plugins we're used to from systems like Jenkins or Wordpress. In order to integrate a plugin one needs to:

  • change the core code of Backstage
  • rebuild the whole codebase and
  • redeploy their IDP

Not exactly the perfect operational flow for a live system that can potentially change multiple times a month. Especially weird now - that we've learned the pains of monolithic deployments and how to mitigate them with modularity.

Upgrades are Scary

So - as we said - configuring plugins and basically adding any other changes to Backstage involves code modifications. This effectively turns your Backstage installation into a fork of the original codebase. Which makes every upgrade a code merge that's on you to perform. And code merges are ... scary.
Backstage developers even created a tool to generate code diffs that one needs to manually integrate in their installation in order to upgrade. Which we're very thankful for. Makes it easier. But still scary.

Getting Everyone On Stage

After you've muscled through all the technical challenges - you hit the hardest part - organizational adoption. At the core of every IDP sits the software catalog - the registry of all the components developed and used at your organization. So for an effective adoption you need:

  1. Collect all that data into Backstage (converting it to Backstage-compatible format)
  2. Persuade all the engineers to consume that data from the IDP and not from wherever they found the data prior to that. (Old habits die hard)
  3. Continuously update the data - so it stays relevant and useful.

For a large org with hundreds of devs and dozens of software components this is no easy feat. More often than not - adoption fails or stumbles. Mainly because there's no true DX (developer experience) expertise in the organization. Backstage itself is just a framework - turning it into a usable and valuable product becomes the job of the team responsible for its rollout. This requires a very specific skillset which is lacking in most places.

So if you've been considering rolling out Backstage at your org - think again... Or -

Use a Backstage Distro

In order to make Backstage adoption and day-to-day operation easier and more valuable for our customers we've been working on our own Backstage distro built to run on Kubernetes.

The goal is to provide an enterprise ready product with:

  • Seamless deployment experience
  • Automated Catalog Discovery
  • Plugin management without rebuilds (for major plugins)
  • Manageable RBAC and enterprise-level auditing out of the box
  • Integrated Observability with customizable metrics.
  • Optional reporting and Finops extensions (once you have everyone in the org in one place, cataloged and documented - makes a lot of sense to generate reports and manage costs accordingly)

Sounds interesting? Feel free to signup at our website to get notified when we release a public beta.

And if the topic of IDPs and Backstage excites you - subscribe to my posts. Here are some of the topics I'm planning to cover:

  • Reviews of proprietary IDP offerings (Port, Cortex, OpsLevel, etc)
  • How to join Backstage OSS community and contribute to Backstage OSS
  • Technical posts on how to do things in Backstage
  • Introduction to Platform Design Principles

I'm also doing a Backstage workshop at Cloud Native Day Switzerland in September, so if you're around - do sign up!

Zaynul Abedin Miah - Jun 28

Items.Title: 什么是 CSS 编写模式：完整指南 | What Is CSS Writing Mode: A Complete Guide
Items.Description:
阿尔萨兰·姆莱克

发表于 6 月 28 日

在 Web 开发领域，层叠样式表 (CSS) 在设计和构建网站方面发挥着至关重要的作用。 在本文中，我们将探讨 CSS 编写模式的概念，了解其不同的价值，并学习如何利用它们来创建具有视觉吸引力且用户友好的网页。

它允许开发人员选择内容应水平显示（从左到右）还是垂直显示（从上到下）。 在设计多语言网站或为非拉丁脚本创建布局时，此属性特别有用。

了解 CSS 写入模式值

让我们仔细看看一些常用的值：

该值适用于大多数水平布局的网站，其中文本和其他元素遵循传统的西方阅读顺序。

 .example-element { writing-mode: horizontal-tb; }

它通常用于中文、日语和韩语等传统上垂直书写的语言。

 .example-element { writing-mode: vertical-rl; }

与“vertical-rl”类似，“vertical-lr”值将书写模式设置为垂直。 然而，内容是从上到下、从左到右流动的。 该值通常用于蒙古语或东南亚使用的某些文字。

 .example-element { writing-mode: vertical-lr; }

该模式主要用于西方语言的竖排文本，例如标题或注释。

 .example-element { writing-mode: sideways-rl; }

该值也适用于西方语言的竖排文本。

 .example-element { writing-mode: sideways-lr; }

应用CSS书写模式

要应用 CSS 编写模式，您需要定位所需的 HTML 元素并指定适当的值。 以下是如何执行此操作的示例：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <style> .example-element { writing-mode: vertical-rl; } </style> </head> <body> <h1>writing mode in css</h1> <p class="example-element">This is a paragraph.</p> </body> </html>

在上面的示例中，类“example-element”的内容将从上到下、从右到左流动。

用CSS书写模式改进网页设计

通过利用不同的书写模式值，您可以创建独特且引人入胜的布局，从而增强用户体验。 以下是使用 CSS 编写模式改进网页设计的一些技巧：

如果您正在设计一个支持多种语言的网站，CSS 编写模式可以让您以最合适的方向呈现内容。 考虑对垂直脚本使用“vertical-rl”，对水平脚本使用“horizo​​ntal-tb”，以确保最佳的可读性。

例如，您可以使用“sideways-rl”或“sideways-lr”来旋转文本元素，为您的设计提供独特的艺术感。

通过CSS编写模式，您可以摆脱传统布局并尝试非常规设计。 垂直文本元素或横向流可以为您的网页添加新鲜和动态的外观，使它们在竞争中脱颖而出。

结论

通过理解不同的价值观并深思熟虑地应用它们，您可以创建视觉上令人惊叹的网站，有效地传达您的信息。 无论您是在开发多语言网站、书法风格的设计还是创新布局，CSS 书写模式都可以让您打造引人入胜且用户友好的 Web 体验。

弗朗西斯科·丘拉 - 6 月 11 日

阿努拉达·阿加瓦尔 - 6 月 18 日

原文:

Arsalan Mlaik

Posted on Jun 28

 

In the world of web development, Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) play a crucial role in designing and structuring websites. One powerful feature of CSS is its writing mode property, which provides developers with the ability to control the layout and direction of content. In this article, we will explore the concept of CSS writing mode, understand its different values, and learn how to utilize them to create visually appealing and user-friendly web pages.

Introduction to CSS Writing Mode

CSS writing mode determines the direction in which content flows within a web page. It allows developers to choose whether content should be displayed horizontally (left to right) or vertically (top to bottom). This property is especially useful when designing multilingual websites or creating layouts for non-Latin scripts.

Understanding CSS Writing Mode Values

CSS writing mode offers various values that can be applied to elements. Let’s take a closer look at some commonly used values:

1. Horizontal-TB (Horizontal Top to Bottom) The “horizontal-tb” value, which is the default, defines the content flow from left to right and top to bottom. This value is suitable for most websites with horizontal layouts, where text and other elements follow the traditional Western reading order.

.example-element {
  writing-mode: horizontal-tb;
}

2. Vertical-RL (Vertical Right to Left) The “vertical-rl” value enables vertical writing mode, where content flows from top to bottom and right to left. It is commonly used for languages such as Chinese, Japanese, and Korean, which are traditionally written vertically.

.example-element {
  writing-mode: vertical-rl;
}

3. Vertical-LR (Vertical Left to Right)

Similar to “vertical-rl,” the “vertical-lr” value sets the writing mode to vertical. However, content flows from top to bottom and left to right. This value is often employed for Mongolian or certain scripts used in Southeast Asia.

.example-element {
  writing-mode: vertical-lr;
}

4. Sideways-RL (Sideways Right to Left) The “sideways-rl” value rotates the content 90 degrees clockwise, making it flow from top to bottom and right to left. This mode is mainly used for vertical text in Western languages, such as headings or annotations.

.example-element {
  writing-mode: sideways-rl;
}

5. Sideways-LR (Sideways Left to Right) Contrary to “sideways-rl,” the “sideways-lr” value rotates the content 90 degrees counterclockwise, resulting in top-to-bottom and left-to-right flow. This value is also applicable for vertical text in Western languages.

.example-element {
  writing-mode: sideways-lr;
}

Applying CSS Writing Mode

To apply CSS writing mode, you need to target the desired HTML element and specify the appropriate value. Here’s an example of how you can do this:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<style>
.example-element {
  writing-mode: vertical-rl;
}
</style>
</head>
<body>

<h1>writing mode in css</h1>
<p class="example-element">This is a paragraph.</p>
</body>
</html>

In the above example, the class “example-element” will have its content flow from top to bottom and right to left.

Improving Web Design with CSS Writing Mode

CSS writing mode offers immense possibilities for web designers. By utilizing different writing mode values, you can create unique and engaging layouts that enhance the user experience. Here are some tips to improve your web design using CSS writing mode:

1. Multilingual Websites
If you’re designing a website that caters to multiple languages, CSS writing mode allows you to present content in the most appropriate orientation. Consider using “vertical-rl” for vertical scripts or “horizontal-tb” for horizontal scripts to ensure optimal readability.

2. Calligraphy and Typography
CSS writing mode can be leveraged to create visually appealing calligraphy or typography effects. For instance, you can use “sideways-rl” or “sideways-lr” to rotate text elements, providing a unique and artistic touch to your design.

3. Creative Layouts

With CSS writing mode, you can break free from traditional layouts and experiment with unconventional designs. Vertical text elements or sideways flow can add a fresh and dynamic look to your web pages, making them stand out from the competition.

Conclusion

CSS writing mode is a powerful tool in a web developer’s arsenal, allowing precise control over content layout and direction. By understanding the different values and applying them thoughtfully, you can create visually stunning websites that effectively communicate your message. Whether you’re working on multilingual sites, calligraphy-inspired designs, or innovative layouts, CSS writing mode empowers you to craft engaging and user-friendly web experiences.

Items.Title: 如何监视班级 | How To Spy on Classes
Items.Description:
在 Jest 中进行间谍活动允许我们模拟类，同时保持原始实现完整。 然而，监视的过程可能会有所不同，特别是在处理类方法与简单函数时。 我们需要处理三种主要场景：监视静态方法、监视单个实例的方法以及监视类的所有实例的方法。

监视静态方法

静态方法与类本身相关联，而不是与类的任何特定实例相关联。 这使得监视他们变得相对简单。 我们只需引用该类并指定我们想要监视的静态方法。

 class Person { static hello() { return 'Hello, I am a static method.'; } goodbye() { return 'Goodbye, I am an instance method.'; } } test('should call static method', () => { // Spy on the static method const spy = jest.spyOn(Person, 'hello'); // Invoke the static method Person.hello(); // Test if the static method has been called expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled(); });

监视单个实例的方法

在我们有权访问该类的特定实例的情况下，我们可以对该实例的任何方法设置间谍。 我们只需引用我们想要监视的实例和方法。

 class Person { static hello() { return 'Hello, I am a static method.'; } goodbye() { return 'Goodbye, I am an instance method.'; } } test('should call instance method of single object', () => { // Create an instance of the class const person = new Person(); // Spy on the instance method const spy = jest.spyOn(person, 'goodbye'); // Invoke the method on the instance person.goodbye(); // Test if the method on the instance has been called expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled(); });

监视所有实例的方法

在许多现实情况下，我们可能无法访问我们想要监视的类的特定实例。 该实例可能隐藏在我们正在测试的模块中，并且不能直接导出或从我们的测试用例访问。 在这种情况下，我们可能会选择监视一个类的所有实例。 为此，我们引用类的原型和我们想要监视的方法。

 class Person { static hello() { return 'Hello, I am a static method.'; } goodbye() { return 'Goodbye, I am an instance method.'; } } // Create an instance and call the instance method function goodbye() { const person = new Person(); return person.goodbye(); } test('should call instance method of all objects', () => { // Spy on the instance method for all instances const spy = jest.spyOn(Person.prototype, 'goodbye'); // Call function without access to underlying instance goodbye(); goodbye(); // Test if the method has been called on any instance expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2); });

我希望这篇文章对您有所帮助。 如果您有任何问题或意见，请随时在下面留下。 如果您想与我联系，可以在LinkedIn或GitHub上找到我。 谢谢阅读！

沙里克·侯赛因·卡兹米 - 6 月 19 日

原文:

 

Spying in Jest allows us to mock classes while keeping the original implementation intact. A spy can help us verify if a certain method was invoked, without interfering with its functionality. However, the process of spying can vary, especially when dealing with class methods versus simple functions. There are three main scenarios we need to deal with: spying on static methods, spying on methods of a single instance, and spying on methods of all instances of a class.

Spying on Static Methods

Static methods are associated with the class itself, not with any specific instance of the class. This makes spying on them relatively straightforward. We simply reference the class and specify which static method we want to spy on.

class Person { static hello() { return 'Hello, I am a static method.'; } goodbye() { return 'Goodbye, I am an instance method.'; }
}
test('should call static method', () => { // Spy on the static method const spy = jest.spyOn(Person, 'hello'); // Invoke the static method Person.hello(); // Test if the static method has been called expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled();
});

Spying on Methods of a Single Instance

In scenarios where we have access to a particular instance of the class, we can set a spy on any of this instance's methods. We simply reference the instance and the method we want to spy on.

class Person { static hello() { return 'Hello, I am a static method.'; } goodbye() { return 'Goodbye, I am an instance method.'; }
} test('should call instance method of single object', () => { // Create an instance of the class const person = new Person(); // Spy on the instance method const spy = jest.spyOn(person, 'goodbye'); // Invoke the method on the instance person.goodbye(); // Test if the method on the instance has been called expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled();
});

Spying on Methods of All Instances

In many real-world cases, we may not have access to the specific instance of the class we want to spy on. This instance might be hidden in a module we are testing and not directly exported or accessible from our test case. In such situations, we may choose to spy on all instances of a class. To do this, we reference the prototype of the class and the method we want to spy.

class Person { static hello() { return 'Hello, I am a static method.'; } goodbye() { return 'Goodbye, I am an instance method.'; }
} // Create an instance and call the instance method
function goodbye() { const person = new Person(); return person.goodbye();
} test('should call instance method of all objects', () => { // Spy on the instance method for all instances const spy = jest.spyOn(Person.prototype, 'goodbye'); // Call function without access to underlying instance goodbye(); goodbye(); // Test if the method has been called on any instance expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2);
});

I hope you found this post helpful. If you have any questions or comments, feel free to leave them below. If you'd like to connect with me, you can find me on LinkedIn or GitHub. Thanks for reading!

Shariq Hussain Kazmi - Jun 19

Items.Title: Dodo IS 开发人员前往现场的 6 个理由 | 6 reasons why Dodo IS developers go to Gemba
Items.Description:
作为一名 QA 工程师，我坚信 Gemba 是了解您的产品的绝佳机会。 它有助于回答这个问题：“我是否在胡说八道？” 今天我想告诉大家更多关于它的优点以及我们参观披萨店后得到的见解。

据介绍，必须来到现场——工作流程进行的地方，收集事实，并直接在现场做出决定，才能充分了解情况。

维基百科

我会从头开始讲为什么我们需要现场。 我们的产品是 Dodo IS — 信息系统，它将我们业务的所有部分结合在一起：

如果不知道如何使用以及谁在使用它，就很难创造出高质量的产品。 为了评估质量，我们使用两种方法：

这种方法在现实生活中非常容易使用。 和所有顾客一样，我们在乘坐地铁、在雨中行走、在 -30C 的温度下或躺在海滩上时都会点披萨。 缺陷、错误和糟糕的用户体验很容易被感觉到。

但在我们的例子中，它意味着更深入地沉浸在产品和环境中，因为你不能随时去当披萨制作者或成为披萨店的经理来了解系统是如何工作的。

谁应该去现场

没有人必须这样做，但每个人都可以。 去现场是一种权利。 没有人禁止去披萨店、咖啡店或烤肉店，但也没有人强迫你。 测试人员、开发人员和产品经理都可以去那里。 最主要的是焦点的不同。

在比萨店里，他们找到想法，获得见解，并能看到全貌。 产品经理使用 Gemba 进行假设检验、深度访谈、客户开发、客户旅程映射等。

这样做的副作用是产品开发的见解和想法不受控制。 从现场回来后，没有一个工程师说这是浪费时间。

为什么要去现场

去现场很容易实施。 开发人员前往比萨饼店或咖啡店并使用该产品。 就这样。 这比在办公室模拟比萨饼店或咖啡店，邀请比萨饼店的实际员工，并在真空中的球形牛的条件下虚构地展示他们的用户体验更容易。

可以有关于业务流程自动化的见解。 我们的一位 Android 开发人员去了 Gemba，了解了标记过程。 员工正在用手写标签计算产品的有效期。 对于每种产品，他们都会计算并写下除霜的开始日期、有效期和失效日期。 产品标签是一个无聊的例行过程，其中经常会出现粗心的错误。

开发人员意识到这绝对是胡说八道。 要实现自动化，“程序员需要半个小时的工作”，所以他继续将其自动化。 这就是打印标签的移动应用程序的诞生。 操作简化为以下内容：员工在平板电脑上选择一个标题，点击一下，然后打印出包含所有术语的标签，仅此而已。

在现场，产品的缺陷更加明显。 这些功能是为平板电脑开发的。 在审核过程中，员工可以直接在平板电脑中输入数据，但冷冻室和冰箱里太冷，平板电脑拒绝工作，有时会失去连接，而且界面本身也不是很方便。 这就是为什么审计结果会写在纸上，然后输入 Dodo IS 中。 双倍工作！

在重写审核器界面之前，开发团队连夜修改以了解流程。 他们带着改进的想法回来了。 例如，如果连接丢失，那么信息的输入必须异步，数据必须存储在本地存储中，并在连接恢复时发送到服务器。

夜间。 与此同时，开发商正在数西红柿

在比萨店中，您可以看到使用应用程序的工作条件。 其中一个开发团队创建了 Pizzeria Tracking。 它是一套六块平板电脑，显示披萨、添加或删除配料的食谱，以及披萨当前在厨房的位置。

面团擀压片

包装片剂

该团队在一家比萨店打开了新的跟踪系统，并去看看它是如何工作的。 在比萨店里，开发人员看到比萨饼制作者急于快速敲击平板电脑，而且他们的手沾满了酱汁或奶酪。 因此，并不总是能够第一次点击平板电脑。 团队非常惊讶。 当然，在办公室里，我们用干净的手点击了平板电脑，并不着急。 去了现场后，团队回来重新设计了敲击方法，这样用户即使在手很脏的情况下也可以快速标记产品。

去比萨店后，您会提供最合适的解决方案。 您知道业务是如何运作的，以及现实生活中流程是如何设置的。 当业务向你提出问题时，对业务有广阔的视野并了解系统内部是如何工作的，你可以为问题提供更简单或更系统的解决方案。

快乐的现场开发者

您可以看到产品的难以访问或复杂的界面如何影响员工和客户。 你站在收银台，速度越来越慢，有人排队，有些顾客变得紧张或离开。 如果没有这些宝贵的知识，开发人员可能不会注意到响应延迟的增加。

我们的 CTO @pritchin很高兴为您送披萨

为什么不去现场

我从来无法想象这种方法有什么缺点，除了一个：我们去现场的开发人员在经济上不合理。 我们收到很多反馈说我们正在做昂贵的废话，我们付钱给开发人员旋转披萨，分散了他对工作和当前任务的注意力。 我的观点很简单——我们为开发人员的工作和产品付费。 他们只是在比萨店工作，以客户的眼光看待产品，并实时回答自己的问题“我们是否做了废话？”。

结束

在 Dodo Brands，去现场是一件很平常的事情。 这是一次很酷的体验，它可以帮助我们检查我们是否生产出高质量的产品。

我喜欢我们的一位开发人员所写的：“对我来说，作为一名从未去过现场的开发人员，去过现场的开发人员看起来就像是秘密知识的持有者。 在股东大会的讨论中，这样的人可能会说：“你提出的功能不会起作用，因为我在一家比萨店里，看到了它的真实情况。 一般来说，你至少需要去现场才能做到这一点”。

关于现场和 IT 人员的两个最常见问题

你会在没有医疗证明的情况下去披萨店吗？ 决不。 没有医疗证明就不能去现场。 我们是普通人，严格遵守清洁标准。 不能有任何例外。 在您前往现场之前，您需要通过体检并获得适当的工作许可证。

开发商需要清理厕所吗？ 我们去披萨店是为了了解我们的产品在“战斗”条件下如何工作。 但当我们来到披萨店时，我们就成为了正式的实习生，所以我们仍然可以被要求打扫卫生，我们也会做。



原文:

As a QA engineer, I strongly believe that Gemba is a great opportunity for understanding your product. It helps to answer the question: “Am I doing bullshit or not?” Today I want to tell you more about its advantages and the insights that we got after visiting a pizzeria.

What does Gemba stand for

Gemba (Japanese 現場), Genchi Genbutsu (Japanese 現地現物) translates as “the real location, real thing” (meaning “the situation onsite”) denotes an approach specific to the Japanese kaizen management practice. According to it, it is necessary to come to Gemba — the place where the work process is performed, collect the facts, and make a decision directly on the spot in order to fully understand the situation.

Wikipedia

I will start from the beginning to talk about why we need Gemba. Our product is the Dodo IS — information system, which unites all parts of our business:

· B2C (website and mobile apps);

· B2B (apps for shift control, point of sale management, warehouse balances, cash register interfaces, etc.)

It is quite difficult to create a high-quality product without knowing how it is used and who works with it. To assess the quality, we use 2 approaches:

· Eat your own dog food (for B2C) — a method of probing user experience when employees in a company use the products they produce. This approach is quite easy to use in real life. Like all customers, we order pizza when we are going by subway, walking in the rain, at -30C, or lying on the beach. Flaws, bugs, and bad UX are easy to feel.

· Gemba (for B2B) — a method of probing user experience when employees in a company use the products they develop. But in our case, it implies a deeper immersion into the product and the environment because you cannot go to work as a pizza maker at any time or become a manager in a pizzeria in order to understand how the system works.

Who should go to Gemba

No one has to, but everyone can. Going to Gemba is a right. No one forbids going to a pizzeria, coffee shop, or doner, but no one forces you either. Testers, developers, and product managers can go there. The main thing about it is the difference in focus.

· For products, this is one of the main activities. In a pizzeria, they find ideas, get insights, and can see the whole picture. Product managers use Gemba for hypothesis testing, in-depth interviews, customer development, customer journey mapping, and so on.

· Developers and QA engineers go to Gemba with the aim of “touching” the product they have made. A side effect of this is uncontrolled insights and ideas for product development. After returning from Gemba, not a single engineer said that it was a waste of time.

Why go to Gemba

1. Cheap and cheerful

Going to Gemba is easy to implement. The developer goes to a pizzeria or coffee shop and uses the product. That’s all. This is easier than simulating a pizzeria or coffee shop in the office, inviting actual employees of the pizzeria, and fictitiously demonstrating their user experience in the conditions of a spherical cow in a vacuum.

2. Automation Insights

There can be insights about business process automation. One of our Android developers went to Gemba and saw what the marking process was like. Employees were COUNTING IN HEAD and HANDWRITING labels with the expiry dates of the products. For each product, they count and write the start date of defrosting, the validity date, and the expiry date. Product labeling is a boring routine process in which careless mistakes constantly occur.

The developer realized that this was absolute bullshit. To automate this was “half an hour of a programmer’s work,” so he went ahead and automated it. This is how the mobile application for printing labels was born. The operation is reduced to the following: an employee selects a heading on the tablet, makes a tap, and a label with all the terms is printed, that’s it.

3. Flaws in the field

In Gemba, the defects of the product are more evident. Dodo IS has features for auditing. These features were developed for the tablet. During the audit, an employee can enter data directly into the tablet, but it is too cold in the freezer and refrigerator, the tablet refuses to work, sometimes the connection is lost, and the interface itself is not very convenient. That’s why the results of the audit are written down on paper, and entered into Dodo IS later. Double work!

Before rewriting the interface of the auditor, the development team went to night revisions in order to understand the process. They returned with ideas for improvement. For example, if the connection is lost, then the input of information must be made asynchronous, the data must be stored in local storage, and sent to the server when the connection is restored.

Nighttime. Meanwhile, the developers are counting the tomatoes

4. Real working conditions

In a pizzeria, you can see the working conditions in which the applications are used. One of the development teams created Pizzeria Tracking. It's a set of six tablets displaying the pizza, the recipe with ingredients added or removed, and the location of the pizza in the kitchen at the moment.

Dough rolling tablet

Packing tablet

The team turned on the new tracking in one of the pizzerias and went to see how it worked. In the pizzeria, the developers saw that pizza makers were in a hurry to tap on the tablet very quickly, and they were doing it with their hands dirty with sauce or cheese. As a result, it was not always possible to tap on the tablet the first time. The team was very surprised. Surely, in the office, we clicked on the tablet with clean hands and there was no hurry. After going to Gemba, the team got back and redesigned the tap method in such a way, so that users could mark products even when they were doing it very quickly and with dirty hands.

5. Problem-solving

After going to a pizzeria, you offer the most suitable solutions. You know how the business works, how processes are set up in real life. When the business comes to you with a problem, having a broad view of the business and knowing how the system works inside, you can offer a simpler or more systematic solution to the problem.

Happy developers in Gemba

6. Product Impact

You can see how the inaccessibility or complex interface of the product affects employees and customers. You are standing at the checkout, and it is getting slow, there is a queue, and some customers are getting nervous or leaving. Without this valuable knowledge, the developer might not have noticed the increase in response latency.

QA engineer is taking orders at the checkout

Our CTO @pritchin is happy to deliver your pizza

Why not go to Gemba

I have never been able to imagine any disadvantages of this approach except for one: our developers who go to Gemba are not financially justified. We receive a lot of feedback that we are doing expensive bullshit, we are paying a developer for spinning pizza, distracting him from work and his immediate tasks. My opinion is simple — we pay developers for their work, for their product. They just perform work in a pizzeria, look at the product with the eyes of a client and answer themselves in real-time to the question “Have we done bullshit or not?”.

The End

At Dodo Brands, going to Gemba is a common thing. This is a cool experience, which helps us to check that we make a high-quality product.

I liked how one of our developers wrote: “For me, as a developer who has never gone to Gemba, a developer who went to Gemba looks like a bearer of secret knowledge. During a discussion at a general meeting, such a person may say: “The feature you propose will not work, because I was in a pizzeria and saw how it really was there. In general, you need to go to Gemba at least for this”.

Two most common questions about Gemba and IT people

Do you go to a pizzeria without a medical certificate? No way. You can’t go to Gemba without a medical certificate. We are normal people and strictly observe the standards of cleanliness. There can be no exceptions. Before you go to Gemba, you need to pass a medical examination and obtain the appropriate work permit.

Do developers need to clean the toilets? We go to pizzerias in order to understand how our product works in “combat” conditions. But when we come to a pizzeria, we become full-fledged trainees, so we can still be asked to clean up, and we will do it.
Items.Author: evgeniyskt

Items.Title: JavaFX 中的阿拉伯语文本呈现问题 | Arabic Text Rendering Issues in JavaFX
Items.Description:
阿卜杜勒拉赫曼·巴尤米

发表于 6 月 28 日

阿拉伯语是从右到左的脚本，在 JavaFX 应用程序中进行渲染时会带来一定的挑战。 开发人员遇到的一个常见问题是阿拉伯文本的字符之间出现空格，这会破坏文本的正确显示。 在本文中，我们将探索解决此问题并确保 JavaFX 中阿拉伯文本正确呈现的解决方案。

例子：

为了说明这些更改的影响，让我们考虑一个示例，其中阿拉伯文本渲染不正确，字符之间有空格。 这是一张演示错误渲染的图像：

应用建议的解决方案后，阿拉伯文本将正确呈现。 这是显示校正后渲染的图像：

解决方案：

要解决 JavaFX 中的阿拉伯文本渲染问题，我们可以按照以下步骤操作：

阿拉伯文本需要特殊的形状来连接字母并形成适当的连字。 通过在JavaFX中启用阿拉伯文本整形，我们可以确保字符正确连接和显示。 这可以通过将系统属性prism.text设置为t2k来完成。 您可以在 JavaFX 应用程序开始时使用以下代码片段以编程方式实现此目的：

 System.setProperty("prism.text", "t2k");

字体渲染也会影响阿拉伯文本的外观。 为了提高文本渲染的平滑度，请确保启用抗锯齿功能。 您可以使用以下代码片段设置抗锯齿属性：

 System.setProperty("prism.lcdtext", "false"); System.setProperty("prism.text", "t2k");

根据您的要求调整抗锯齿属性。

请务必注意，需要在启动 JavaFX 应用程序之前应用这些设置。

这些配置应该放在哪里

请记住在启动 JavaFX 应用程序之前应用这些设置，以确保获得所需的渲染行为。 完成这些更改后，您可以为阿拉伯语用户提供更好的用户体验，并增强 JavaFX 应用程序的本地化功能。

 public static void main(String[] args) { // Fix Arabic letters in JavaFX System.setProperty("prism.lcdtext", "false"); System.setProperty("prism.text", "t2k"); // Launch the JavaFX application launch(args); }

这是主类的完整示例

import javafx.application.Application; import javafx.geometry.Insets; import javafx.geometry.Pos; import javafx.scene.Scene; import javafx.scene.control.Label; import javafx.scene.layout.VBox; import javafx.stage.Stage; public class Main extends Application { @Override public void start(Stage primaryStage) { // Create a label Label labelArabic = new Label("هذا نص عربي للتجربة"); labelArabic.setStyle("-fx-font-size: 70px;-fx-font-weight: bold;"); // Create a VBox as the root node VBox vBox = new VBox(labelArabic); vBox.setAlignment(Pos.CENTER); vBox.setPadding(new Insets(50)); vBox.setSpacing(20); // Create a scene and place it in the stage primaryStage.setScene(new Scene(vBox)); // show the stage primaryStage.show(); } public static void main(String[] args) { // Fix Arabic letters in JavaFX System.setProperty("prism.lcdtext", "false"); System.setProperty("prism.text", "t2k"); // Launch the JavaFX application launch(args); } }

一旦解除暂停，abdelrahmanbayoumi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，abdelrahmanbayoumi 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 abdelrahmanbayoumi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 abdelrahmanbayoumi：

使 abdelrahmanbayoumi 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

坚持不懈的 abdelrahmanbayoumi 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Abdelrahman Bayoumi

Posted on Jun 28

 

Arabic, being a right-to-left script, poses certain challenges when it comes to rendering in JavaFX applications. One common issue that developers encounter is Arabic text appearing with spaces between the characters, which disrupts the proper display of the text. In this article, we will explore a solution to resolve this problem and ensure correct rendering of Arabic text in JavaFX.

Example:

To illustrate the impact of these changes, let's consider an example where Arabic text is rendered incorrectly with spaces between the characters. Here is an image demonstrating the incorrect rendering:

After applying the suggested solution, the Arabic text will be rendered correctly. Here is an image showcasing the corrected rendering:

Solution:

To resolve Arabic text rendering issues in JavaFX, we can follow these steps:

1- Enable Arabic text shaping:

Arabic text requires special shaping to connect the letters and form the appropriate ligatures. By enabling Arabic text shaping in JavaFX, we can ensure that the characters are correctly connected and displayed. This can be done by setting the system property prism.text to t2k. You can achieve this programmatically at the beginning of your JavaFX application using the following code snippet:

System.setProperty("prism.text", "t2k");

2- Configure font rendering settings:

Font rendering can also impact the appearance of Arabic text. To improve the smoothness of text rendering, ensure that anti-aliasing is enabled. You can set the anti-aliasing property using the following code snippet:

System.setProperty("prism.lcdtext", "false");
System.setProperty("prism.text", "t2k");

Adjust the anti-aliasing property as needed based on your requirements.

It's important to note that these settings need to be applied before launching your JavaFX application.

Where to put these configurations

Remember to apply these settings before launching your JavaFX application to ensure the desired rendering behavior. With these changes in place, you can provide a better user experience for Arabic-speaking users and enhance the localization capabilities of your JavaFX applications.

public static void main(String[] args) { // Fix Arabic letters in JavaFX System.setProperty("prism.lcdtext", "false"); System.setProperty("prism.text", "t2k"); // Launch the JavaFX application launch(args);
}

This is a full example of the Main Class 

import javafx.application.Application;
import javafx.geometry.Insets;
import javafx.geometry.Pos;
import javafx.scene.Scene;
import javafx.scene.control.Label;
import javafx.scene.layout.VBox;
import javafx.stage.Stage; public class Main extends Application { @Override public void start(Stage primaryStage) { // Create a label Label labelArabic = new Label("هذا نص عربي للتجربة"); labelArabic.setStyle("-fx-font-size: 70px;-fx-font-weight: bold;"); // Create a VBox as the root node VBox vBox = new VBox(labelArabic); vBox.setAlignment(Pos.CENTER); vBox.setPadding(new Insets(50)); vBox.setSpacing(20); // Create a scene and place it in the stage primaryStage.setScene(new Scene(vBox)); // show the stage primaryStage.show(); } public static void main(String[] args) { // Fix Arabic letters in JavaFX System.setProperty("prism.lcdtext", "false"); System.setProperty("prism.text", "t2k"); // Launch the JavaFX application launch(args); } }

Items.Title: 服务器监控服务如何提高网站的性能 | How a Server Monitoring Service Can Improve Your Website's Performance
Items.Description:
介绍

在当今的数字环境中，拥有强大的在线影响力对于企业至关重要。 精心设计和优化的网站可以成为宝贵的资产，吸引潜在客户并推动转化。 然而，经常被忽视的一个方面是网站的性能。 页面加载缓慢、频繁停机和其他性能问题可能会对用户体验产生负面影响，并最终损害您的业务。

为了解决这些问题，企业需要有效的服务器监控服务，可以持续监控其网站的性能并确保最佳功能。 在本文中，我们将探讨服务器监控服务的重要性以及它如何显着提高网站的性能。

网站性能的意义

网站性能在提供积极的用户体验方面起着至关重要的作用。 研究表明，用户对加载缓慢的网站缺乏耐心，大多数人希望页面在几秒钟内加载。 如果一个网站无法满足这些期望，访问者可能会放弃它并寻求替代方案。 这可能会导致更高的跳出率、更低的转化率以及潜在客户的流失。

此外，网站性能是搜索引擎排名的重要因素。 谷歌等主要搜索引擎将页面速度视为排名因素，这意味着速度慢的网站可能很难在搜索结果中占据显着位置。 为了确保您的网站排名良好并吸引自然流量，必须优先考虑其性能。

服务器监控服务的作用

服务器监控服务充当您网站的警惕守护者，不断监控其性能并提醒您可能出现的任何问题。 通过主动识别和解决性能瓶颈，服务器监控服务可以显着增强网站的功能和用户体验。 以下是使用服务器监控服务的一些主要好处：

可靠的服务器监控服务可以实时监控您网站的性能。 它跟踪各种指标，例如页面加载时间、服务器响应时间和正常运行时间。 借助这些宝贵的数据，您可以确定需要改进的领域并立即采取措施纠正任何性能问题。

网站停机可能会对您的业务造成损害，导致收入损失和声誉受损。 服务器监控服务可以在您的网站遇到问题或离线时立即向您发出警报，从而帮助防止停机。 通过及时解决这些问题，您可以最大限度地减少停机时间并确保用户不间断地访问您的网站。

服务器监控服务可以深入了解可能减慢网站速度的性能瓶颈。 通过分析这些数据，您可以识别需要优化的区域，并采取适当的措施来提高网站的速度和响应能力。 这种优化可以带来更快的加载时间、改善的用户体验和更高的搜索引擎排名。

网站安全是在数字领域运营的企业最关心的问题。 服务器监控服务可以帮助识别安全漏洞，例如恶意软件攻击或未经授权的访问尝试，并提醒您潜在的威胁。 通过主动解决这些安全问题，您可以保护您的网站和用户的敏感数据。

选择正确的服务器监控服务

既然我们了解了服务器监控服务的重要性，那么选择适合您的特定需求的服务至关重要。 选择服务器监控服务时需要考虑以下几个因素：

确保您选择的服务器监控服务提供全面的监控功能。 它应该能够监控各种性能指标，包括服务器响应时间、页面加载速度、数据库性能等等。 监控功能越广泛，您就越有能力识别和解决潜在问题。

寻找提供实时警报和通知的服务器监控服务。 提示警报使您可以在出现性能问题时立即采取行动，防止长时间停机并最大限度地减少对用户的影响。 可自定义的警报设置可确保您以最适合您偏好的方式接收通知。

有效的服务器监控服务应提供详细的数据报告和分析功能。 通过访问全面的报告，您可以深入了解网站的性能趋势并识别可能影响其整体速度和功能的模式。 这些见解将帮助您做出明智的决策，进一步优化您的网站。

考虑可以根据您的业务需求进行扩展的服务器监控服务。 随着您的网站的发展，您可能需要额外的监控功能或同时监控多个网站的能力。 确保您选择的服务能够满足此类要求，将为您省去将来切换到新提供商的麻烦。

结论

在当今竞争激烈的数字环境中，提供无缝的网站体验对于您的业务成功至关重要。 通过投资可靠的服务器监控服务，您可以增强网站的性能、改善用户体验并提高搜索引擎排名。 正确的服务器监控服务将充当您网站的守护者，确保其平稳运行，最大限度地减少停机时间，并为您提供持续优化的宝贵见解。



原文:

Introduction

In today's digital landscape, having a strong online presence is essential for businesses. A well-designed and optimized website can be a valuable asset, attracting potential customers and driving conversions. However, one aspect that is often overlooked is the performance of the website. Slow-loading pages, frequent downtime, and other performance issues can negatively impact user experience and ultimately harm your business.

To address these concerns, businesses need an effective server monitoring service that can constantly monitor the performance of their website and ensure optimal functionality. In this article, we will explore the importance of a server monitoring service and how it can significantly improve your website's performance.

The Significance of Website Performance

Website performance plays a crucial role in providing a positive user experience. Studies have shown that users have little patience for slow-loading websites, with most expecting a page to load within a few seconds. If a website fails to meet these expectations, visitors are likely to abandon it and seek alternatives. This can lead to higher bounce rates, decreased conversions, and a loss of potential customers.

Additionally, website performance is a vital factor in search engine rankings. Major search engines like Google consider page speed as a ranking factor, which means that a slow website may struggle to achieve a prominent position in search results. To ensure your website ranks well and attracts organic traffic, it is imperative to prioritize its performance.

The Role of a Server Monitoring Service

A server monitoring service acts as a vigilant guardian for your website, constantly monitoring its performance and alerting you to any issues that may arise. By proactively identifying and resolving performance bottlenecks, a server monitoring service can significantly enhance your website's functionality and user experience. Here are some key benefits of using a server monitoring service:

1. Real-time Performance Monitoring

A reliable server monitoring service provides real-time monitoring of your website's performance. It keeps track of various metrics, such as page load times, server response times, and uptime. With this valuable data at your disposal, you can identify areas for improvement and take prompt action to rectify any performance issues.

2. Downtime Prevention

Website downtime can be detrimental to your business, leading to lost revenue and a damaged reputation. A server monitoring service can help prevent downtime by immediately alerting you when your website experiences issues or goes offline. By addressing these problems promptly, you can minimize downtime and ensure uninterrupted access to your website for your users.

3. Performance Optimization

A server monitoring service provides insights into the performance bottlenecks that may be slowing down your website. By analyzing this data, you can identify areas that need optimization and take appropriate measures to enhance your website's speed and responsiveness. This optimization can result in faster loading times, improved user experience, and higher search engine rankings.

4. Security Enhancement

Website security is a paramount concern for businesses operating in the digital realm. A server monitoring service can help identify security vulnerabilities, such as malware attacks or unauthorized access attempts, and alert you to potential threats. By addressing these security issues proactively, you can protect your website and the sensitive data of your users.

Choosing the Right Server Monitoring Service

Now that we understand the significance of a server monitoring service, it is crucial to choose the right one for your specific needs. Here are a few factors to consider when selecting a server monitoring service:

*1. Comprehensive Monitoring Capabilities
*
Ensure that the server monitoring service you choose offers comprehensive monitoring capabilities. It should be able to monitor various performance metrics, including server response times, page load speeds, database performance, and more. The more extensive the monitoring capabilities, the better equipped you will be to identify and resolve potential issues.

2. Real-time Alerts and Notifications

Look for a server monitoring service that provides real-time alerts and notifications. Prompt alerts allow you to take immediate action when performance issues arise, preventing prolonged downtime and minimizing the impact on your users. Customizable alert settings ensure that you receive notifications in the manner that suits your preferences best.

*3. Data Reporting and Analysis
*
An effective server monitoring service should offer detailed data reporting and analysis features. Access to comprehensive reports allows you to gain insights into your website's performance trends and identify patterns that may be affecting its overall speed and functionality. These insights will help you make informed decisions to optimize your website further.

4. Scalability and Flexibility

Consider a server monitoring service that can scale with your business needs. As your website grows, you may require additional monitoring capabilities or the ability to monitor multiple websites simultaneously. Ensuring that your chosen service can accommodate such requirements will save you the hassle of switching to a new provider in the future.

Conclusion

In today's competitive digital landscape, delivering a seamless website experience is crucial for the success of your business. By investing in a reliable server monitoring service, you can enhance your website's performance, improve user experience, and boost search engine rankings. The right server monitoring service will act as your website's guardian, ensuring its smooth operation, minimizing downtime, and providing you with valuable insights for continuous optimization.

Items.Title: 在 Package.json 中组织大量脚本 | Organizing a Large Number of Scripts in Package.json
Items.Description:
当需要管理项目的 package.json 文件中的大量脚本时，导航很快就会变得困难。 不过，您可以采取一些简单的步骤来使脚本更有条理、更易于阅读。 在这个简短的教程中，我们将探讨如何添加分隔符可以提高 package.json 的可读性。在脚本对象中使用分隔符有助于创建清晰的部分并将相关脚本分组在一起。 让我们通过几个简单的步骤来完成：

没有分隔符的“Scripts”对象：

 "scripts": { "help": "scripts-help -w 40", "tsc": "tsc", "tscwatch": "tsc --watch", "serve": "serve ./site/" },

使用npm run打印可用命令：

通过npm run-script可以在dev-tips@1.0.0中使用脚本：


 help scripts-help -w 40 tsc tsc tscwatch tsc --watch serve serve ./site/

但是当像这样使用分隔符时：

 "scripts": { "help": "scripts-help -w 40", "\n========== Building ==========": "", "tsc": "tsc", "tscwatch": "tsc --watch", "\n========== Serving ==========": "", "serve": "serve ./site/" },

可用命令以更易读的形式打印：

﻿
通过npm run-script可以在dev-tips@1.0.0中使用脚本：

 help scripts-help -w 40 ========== Building ========== tsc tsc tscwatch tsc --watch ========== Serving ========== serve serve ./site/﻿

查看Github 存储库。

﻿

作者： Bright Inventions后端开发人员 Radosław Landowski

原文:

When it comes to managing a large number of scripts in your project's package.json file, it can quickly become challenging to navigate. However, there are simple steps you can take to make your scripts more organized and easier to read. In this short tutorial, we'll explore how adding separators can improve the readability of your package.json. ﻿ Using separators in your scripts object helps create clear sections and group related scripts together. Let’s do it in a few simple steps:

“Scripts” object with no separators: 

"scripts": { "help": "scripts-help -w 40", "tsc": "tsc", "tscwatch": "tsc --watch", "serve": "serve ./site/" },

Prints the available commands with npm run as: ﻿

Scripts available in dev-tips@1.0.0 via npm run-script:


 help scripts-help -w 40 tsc tsc tscwatch tsc --watch serve serve ./site/

But when separators are used like so: 

"scripts": { "help": "scripts-help -w 40", "\n========== Building ==========": "", "tsc": "tsc", "tscwatch": "tsc --watch", "\n========== Serving ==========": "", "serve": "serve ./site/" },

The available commands are printed in a much more readable form:

﻿
Scripts available in dev-tips@1.0.0 via npm run-script:

 help scripts-help -w 40 ========== Building ========== tsc tsc tscwatch tsc --watch ========== Serving ========== serve serve ./site/
﻿

Check out Github repository.

﻿

By Radosław Landowski, Backend Developer @ Bright Inventions

Philip John Basile - Jun 21

Items.Title: 不公平的游戏——当风险投资变成邪恶帝国时 | The Unfair Game - When VC Funding Turns into an Evil Empire
Items.Description:
约翰·拉什

发表于 6 月 27 日

哈喽，朋友们！ 我多年来一直站在黑暗面……呃，对不起，我指的是 VC（风险投资）方面。
我用自己的血汗钱进行投资，并为我的初创公司多次筹集资金。

但现在？ 现在我是自筹资金，就像有人在我脑子里打开了一个灯泡。
我的眼睛比当你不经测试不小心运行代码却发现像忍者一样隐藏在那里的错误时睁得更大！

我意识到，大部分所谓的“风险投资资金”只是在玩我所谓的“不公平游戏”时被烧掉的。 我们谈论的是购买《福布斯》文章、媒体广告、机器人农场和 1000 多个黑客行为，或者正如我们开发人员喜欢说的那样——作弊。

当我自己得到风险投资人的支持时，情况似乎并没有那么不同。 他们像瓷器店里的公牛一样狡猾地把数百万美元放在你的桌子上，同时推动你不惜一切代价快速成长。
但当你转换立场并突然从另一个角度看待事物时……哇，耐莉！

金钱的规则似乎很简单：拥有它的人不仅将其用于乘法表，还用于统治游戏。 那些有粉丝的人会得到更多的粉丝，因为嘿为什么不呢？！ 所以基本上我们的座右铭变成了：“对于拥有某些东西的人，一切都会被给予”。 但请坚持住！

这不是圣经经文，也不是任何公平竞争。

现在请不要误会我的意思；
我并不是说财富分配应该看起来像一个大披萨，每个人都从中得到平等的份额——毕竟资本主义还没有完全消亡，我上次检查过😄——但我们肯定可以做得更好吗？

因为在这种情况下开始做所有事情的玩家越来越少，如果他们喜欢最初的东西，那就太好了，但随着用户群的增长，他们添加更多产品只是因为……好吧……他们可以。

他们喜欢这些新产品吗？ 很可能不会； 它的业务纯粹而简单。

这让我们绕了一圈又回到了起点：

仅靠金钱回报驱动真的是一件好事吗？
由激情而非金钱驱动的梦想也能创造成功的企业吗？

所以今天，亲爱的朋友们，让我们一起开始一个使命：改变事物运作方式的使命； 致力于创建由与他们所做的事情息息相关的团队制作的工具，而不是简单地追逐美元符号！

我们目前正在进行的一场这样的战斗是通过一个名为 unicornplatform 的勇敢项目来对抗另一款由猜猜谁资助的产品？ 我们的老朋友Mr.VC Money Bags本人！

尽管网站流量或社交媒体存在可以忽略不计，而且任何真实产品发布日期的证据为零，但该竞争者还是推出了一个包含候补名单的登陆页面，并神奇地积累了点赞！

是的，在任何人问之前 - 上帝的意志显然也没有延伸到让你的代码正常工作😜

永远记住，生活不仅仅是赢得或输掉战斗，人们有时站起来并与高到足以让杰克和他的豆茎眩晕的可能性进行斗争✊

你已经为自己赢得了一些 Twitter 糖果 🍭 继续关注我@johnrushx除非你希望明天早上在你的门外等待恶业 😉 不要说没有人警告过你！

创建一个简单、强大的产品非常困难。最终看起来很容易，但一开始并不容易。我们制作了最好的人工智能登陆页面构建器！我们统治网站的旅程才刚刚开始。我们为您不间断地工作了六个月。您支持意味着我的世界

亚伦·K·桑德斯 - 3 月 16 日

原文:

John Rush

Posted on Jun 27

 

Hola, amigos! I've been on the dark side... uh excuse me, I mean the VC (Venture Capital) side for years.
Invested my own hard-earned money and raised funds multiple times for my startups.

But now? Now I'm self-funding and it's like someone turned on a light bulb in my head.
My eyes are popping open wider than when you accidentally run your code without testing only to find out there were bugs hiding in there like ninjas!

I realized that most of this so-called "VC money" is just burned playing what I have come to call the "unfair game". We're talking about buying Forbes articles, media ads, bot farms and 1000 more hacks or as we devs love to say - cheats.

When I was backed by VCs myself it didn't seem so different. They put million dollars on your table with all the subtlety of a bull in a china shop while pushing you to grow fast at all costs.
But when you switch sides and suddenly see things from another perspective... woah Nelly!

The rule of money seems simple: those who have it use it not just for multiplication tables but also for domination games. Those with followers get even more followers because hey why not?! So basically our motto becomes: 'to those who have something, everything will be given'. But hold up folks!

This ain't no Bible verse nor is it any kind of fair play.

Now don’t get me wrong here;
I’m not saying wealth distribution should look like one big pizza everyone gets an equal slice from – after all capitalism isn’t quite dead yet last time I checked 😄 – but surely we can do better than this?

Because fewer players start doing everything in this scenario which would be great if they loved their initial stuff but as their user base grows they add more products just because…well…they can.

Do they love these new products? Most likely nope; its business pure and simple.

Which leads us back round full circle to where we started off:

Is being driven by monetary returns alone really such a good thing?
Can dreams powered by passion rather than cash create successful ventures too?

So today dear friends let’s embark together on a mission: A mission towards changing how things work; towards creating tools made by teams that live & breathe what they do rather than simply chasing dollar signs!

One such fight we’re currently engaged in is through one of brave project called unicornplatform against another product funded by guess who? Our old friend Mr.VC Money Bags himself!

This contender has launched a landing page complete with waitlist & magically accumulating upvotes despite having negligible site traffic or social media presence plus zero evidence of any real product launch dates!!

And yes before anyone asks – God’s will apparently doesn’t extend itself towards getting your code working properly either 😜

As always remember life isn’t simply about winning or losing battles folks – sometimes standing up & fighting against odds stacked high enough to give Jack & his beanstalk vertigo counts too ✊

P.S If you read till here then congrats amigo! You’ve earned yourself some Twitter candy 🍭 Go ahead follow me @johnrushx unless you want bad karma waiting outside your door tomorrow morning 😉 Don't say nobody warned ya!!

--

Creating a simple, strong product is crazy hard.It looks easy in the end, but not at first.We made the best AI landing page builder!Our journey to rule websites just started.We worked six months non-stop for you.Your support means the world me

producthunt.com/posts/unicorn-…

Sloan the DEV Moderator - Apr 19

Aaron K Saunders - Mar 16

Items.Title: 每日 Ruby 方法：#count | Ruby Method of the Day: #count
Items.Description:
伯德特·拉马尔

发表于 6 月 28 日

方法count返回集合中符合给定条件的项目数的整数计数。

该方法可用如下：

该方法的调用方式有以下三种：

  • 不带参数且不带块时，返回集合中的项目数：
 [0, 1, 2].count # => 3 [].count # => 0
  • 使用参数object ，返回集合中的 items == object的计数：
 [0, 1, 2, 0.0].count(0) # => 2 [0, 1, 2].count(3) # => 0
  • 使用块，调用集合中每个项目的块； 返回块返回真值的元素的计数：
 [0, 1, 2, 3].count {|element| element > 1} # => 2

原文:

Burdette Lamar

Posted on Jun 28

 

Method count returns an integer count of the number of items in a collection that match a given criterion.

The method is available as:

There are three ways to call the method:

  • With no argument and no block, returns the count of items in the collection: 
[0, 1, 2].count # => 3
[].count # => 0
  • With argument object, returns the count of items == object in the collection: 
[0, 1, 2, 0.0].count(0) # => 2
[0, 1, 2].count(3) # => 0
  • With a block, calls the block with each item in the collection; returns the count of elements for which the block returns a truthy value: 
[0, 1, 2, 3].count {|element| element > 1} # => 2

Items.Title: 如何快速将 Java pojo 类转换为 Record | How to convert a Java pojo class to Record fastly
Items.Description:
埃弗顿弗莱雷

发表于 6 月 28 日

随着 Java 17 的发布，引入了一些新功能，包括类使用分析的增强和 Java 记录功能的引入。 在本文中，我们将深入研究 Java 类的用法并探索新的 Java 记录功能。 此外，我们将在 IntelliJ IDEA 中发现一个新插件，它有助于将类转换为记录，使批量转换高效且轻松。

了解 Java 类的用法：

通过分析类的使用情况，开发人员可以深入了解依赖关系、潜在的重构机会以及对类进行更改的影响。

类使用分析提供了以下几个方面的全面概述：

  1. 引用类：识别引用特定类的类有助于理解代码库中的依赖关系和关系。 它允许开发人员识别修改类对代码其他部分的影响。

  2. 方法调用：分析类上调用的方法有助于理解它的使用方式以及它与其他类的交互。 当考虑重构或修改类时，这些知识很有用。

  3. 继承和实现：分析类继承和接口实现有助于理解类之间的层次结构和关系。 这些信息对于维护代码完整性和制定设计决策至关重要。

要在 Java 中定义记录，请使用“record”关键字，后跟类名和字段列表：

 public record Person(String name, int age) { // Additional methods and behavior can be added here }

简洁性：记录减少了定义简单数据保存类所需的样板代码，从而增强了代码的可读性和可维护性。

  • 不可变性：记录中的所有字段都是隐式最终的，因此默认情况下它们是不可变的。 这种不变性确保了线程安全并有助于避免意外突变。

  • 生成的方法：记录根据声明的字段自动生成有用的方法，例如 equals()、hashCode() 和 toString()，无需手动实现即可轻松使用。

    • 使用IntelliJ IDEA插件进行批量转换：
    随着 Java 17 的发布，IntelliJ IDEA 引入了一个新插件，可以简化类到记录的转换。

    该插件无缝集成到 IDE 中，提供用户友好的界面来选择要转换的文件或包。

    使用该插件很简单：

    1. 在项目结构中选择所需的文件或包。
    2. 右键单击并从上下文菜单中选择“转换为记录”选项。
    3. 该插件分析所选的类并自动将它们转换为记录。
    4. 检查更改并确认转换。

    结论：

    类使用分析并引入了 Java Records 功能，简化了简单数据保存类的定义。 了解类的用法有助于开发人员在重构和维护代码完整性期间做出明智的决策。 此外，IntelliJ IDEA 中的新 Java 记录转换插件简化了将类转换为记录的过程，使批量转换变得轻而易举。 通过利用这些功能和工具，Java 开发人员可以提高代码质量、减少样板代码并提高项目的生产力。

    苏雷什·哈里哈兰 - 6 月 21 日

    原文:

    Everton Freire

    Posted on Jun 28

    Java, being a popular and widely used programming language, continually evolves to meet the demands of modern software development. With the release of Java 17, several new features have been introduced, including enhancements to class usage analysis and the introduction of the Java Records feature. In this article, we will delve into the usage of Java classes and explore the new Java Records feature. Additionally, we will discover a new plugin in IntelliJ IDEA that aids in the transformation of classes to records, making batch conversions efficient and hassle-free.

    Understanding Java Class Usages:

    Java class usage analysis plays a crucial role in identifying how a class is being used within a codebase. By analyzing class usages, developers gain insights into dependencies, potential refactoring opportunities, and the impact of making changes to a class.

    Class usage analysis provides a comprehensive overview of the following aspects:

    1. Referencing Classes: Identifying the classes that reference a specific class helps to understand the dependencies and relationships within the codebase. It allows developers to identify the impact of modifying a class on other parts of the code.

    2. Method Calls: Analyzing the methods called on a class helps in understanding how it is being used and the interactions it has with other classes. This knowledge is beneficial when considering refactoring or making modifications to a class.

    3. Inheritance and Implementations: Analyzing class inheritance and interface implementations aids in understanding the hierarchy and relationships between classes. This information is crucial for maintaining code integrity and making design decisions.

    Introducing Java Records:

    Java 17 introduces the Java Records feature, which provides a concise way to define simple classes that primarily encapsulate immutable data. A Java record automatically generates boilerplate code, including constructor, accessors, equals, hashCode, and toString methods, based on the class's fields.

    To define a record in Java, the 'record' keyword is used, followed by the class name and a list of fields:

    public record Person(String name, int age) { // Additional methods and behavior can be added here
}

    Java Records provide numerous benefits, such as:

    1. Conciseness: Records reduce the boilerplate code required to define simple data-holding classes, enhancing code readability and maintainability.

    2. Immutability: All fields in a record are implicitly final, making them immutable by default. This immutability ensures thread safety and helps avoid accidental mutations.

    3. Generated Methods: Records automatically generate useful methods, such as equals(), hashCode(), and toString(), based on the declared fields, making them readily available without manual implementation.

    Using IntelliJ IDEA Plugin for Batch Conversion:
    IntelliJ IDEA, a popular integrated development environment (IDE), provides excellent support for Java development and offers numerous plugins to enhance productivity. With the release of Java 17, IntelliJ IDEA introduces a new plugin that simplifies the transformation of classes to records.

    The Java Records Transformation Plugin: Pojo2Records in IntelliJ IDEA enables developers to convert multiple classes to records simultaneously, making the migration process efficient and convenient. The plugin seamlessly integrates into the IDE, providing a user-friendly interface to select the files or packages for transformation.

    Using the plugin is straightforward:

    1. Select the desired files or packages in the project structure.
    2. Right-click and choose the "Convert to Record" option from the context menu.
    3. The plugin analyzes the selected classes and automatically converts them to records.
    4. Review the changes and confirm the transformation.

    The Java Records Transformation Plugin significantly reduces the effort and time required to migrate existing classes to records, ensuring a smooth transition while leveraging the benefits of the new Java 17 feature.

    Conclusion:

    Java 17 brings exciting enhancements to

    class usage analysis and introduces the Java Records feature, simplifying the definition of simple data-holding classes. Understanding class usages helps developers make informed decisions during refactoring and maintaining code integrity. Additionally, the new Java Records Transformation Plugin in IntelliJ IDEA streamlines the process of converting classes to records, making batch transformations a breeze. By leveraging these features and tools, Java developers can enhance code quality, reduce boilerplate code, and improve productivity in their projects.

    K M Rejowan Ahmmed - Jun 2

    Suresh Hariharan - Jun 21
